messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "enterprise-gpu-busbar-half-refuted",
 "title": "The 'refuted' GPU-pod doubt was only half-refuted: dense enterprise AI refresh ships on DC busbar, hollowing seg.enterprise from both ends",
 "narrative": "Refinement cycle 1 closed the 'GPU pods skip PDUs' doubt because NVIDIA reference architectures ship PDU variants. But the vendor publishes parallel architectures: the AC/PDU version is the fallback, while the flagship dense design routes power via DC busbar with materially lower PDU attach per rack. The only part of enterprise on-premise that is growing is exactly this GPU-pod build-out; the legacy base is bleeding ~2pp of capacity share per year toward 19% by 2031. So the 0.29 enterprise slice is squeezed twice — the shrinking legacy half keeps buying PDUs it already owns, and the growing AI half is steered toward the substitute. A checkbox marked 'confirmed' is carrying 29% of the TAM.",
 "category": "boundary",
 "targetNodes": [
  "seg.enterprise"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Enterprise on-premise capacity growth is now driven almost entirely by GenAI/GPU infrastructure after a sustained no-growth period, and the flagship reference architecture for the densest pods uses DC busbar with sharply reduced rack-PDU attach, with the AC/PDU variant positioned as legacy fallback",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c19",
    "ledger:seg.enterprise"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "The growing sub-segment of enterprise carries falling PDU content per rack while the legacy on-prem base shrinks ~2pp/year (from 32% end-2025 toward 19% by 2031) — effective enterprise rack-PDU demand falls on both the growth side and the base side simultaneously",
   "refs": [
    "digest:seg-mix#c5",
    "digest:seg-mix#c4"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "seg.enterprise 0.29 (crossCheck marked 'value confirmed') overstates the PDU-addressable enterprise slice; during the venture's 12-month window the realistic value sits below the model's own 0.24 low band, and the funnel's second-largest segment quietly deflates",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:seg.enterprise",
    "digest:seg-mix#c2"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The doubt was explicitly investigated and stamped 'refuted' in refinement cycle 1 — the risk register shows a closed item. The binary confirmed/refuted framing hides that PDU-shipping and PDU-attach-per-MW are different questions: NVIDIA shipping PDU variants does not mean the dense builds that constitute all the segment's growth actually use them.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "Attach-rate evidence from 2026 CE enterprise GPU-pod deployments (integrator BOMs or vendor order data) showing rack-PDU counts per rack at or above the legacy ≥3-per-rack norm in the majority of dense installs.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.4,
  "rationale": "The parallel-architecture steering is documented in vendor reference designs and the enterprise share decline (2pp/year) is a multi-vintage Synergy trend; uncertainty is whether CE enterprises actually deploy the flagship density tier within 12 months or stay on air-cooled AC/PDU designs",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "seg.enterprise",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.2,
   "note": "GPU-driven growth adopts busbar attach while legacy base decays; effective PDU-addressable enterprise share drops below the model's 0.24 low band"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Which power variant (AC/PDU vs DC busbar) is default in NVIDIA/OEM pod reference-architecture documentation revisions",
   "where": "NVIDIA GTC reference-architecture sessions and DGX/MGX pod deployment guides; Dell/HPE AI-factory configurators",
   "threshold": "DC busbar becomes the documented default for ≥50% of published dense-pod configurations",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Enterprise/IT-segment rack-PDU revenue commentary from incumbents",
   "where": "Vertiv and Schneider quarterly earnings calls, rack-power and IT-channel product-line commentary",
   "threshold": "Enterprise PDU line flat or declining while integrated-rack/busbar lines grow double-digit for two consecutive quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Commission an attach-rate teardown of CE enterprise GPU-pod installs (PDUs per rack by density tier) via 5–8 integrator interviews",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "seg.enterprise",
   "note": "Directly re-bases the enterprise slice on measured attach rather than a binary confirmed/refuted flag"
  },
  {
   "action": "Position the Year-1 product for the air-cooled enterprise refresh tier (where AC/PDU remains default) and roadmap a high-amp/DC-compatible SKU for dense pods",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Decouples Year-1 revenue from the sub-segment where substitution bites first"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "boundary-substitution",
 "riskId": "risk.boundary-substitution.enterprise-gpu-busbar-half-refuted"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).

But the vendor publishes parallel architectures: the AC/PDU version is the fallback, while the flagship dense design routes power via DC busbar with materially lower PDU attach per rack. The only part of enterprise on-premise that is growing is exactly this GPU-pod build-out; the legacy base is bleeding ~2pp of capacity share per year toward 19% by 2031.
